user_query:
Please perform a bank reconciliation for April using the two CSVs under input/. Use a $0.50 amount tolerance. Match transactions in layers: first by extracted IDs in the descriptions/memos (e.g., batch IDs like BAT-####, invoice IDs like INV ####, vendor/customer IDs like VEND####/CUST####, and payroll references like PAYRUN ####), allowing 1:1, 1:many, many:1, and grouped many:many when the summed amounts are within tolerance. Then, for any remaining items, group by recognizable payee/payer names and compare summed amounts within tolerance. Keep unmatched items separate.

Read:
- input/bank.csv (bank statement: date, amount, description)
- input/gl.csv (general ledger: date, amount, memo)

Create these files under output/:
1) output/recon_summary.json — JSON with keys: threshold, matched_bank_rows, matched_gl_rows, unreconciled_bank_rows, unreconciled_gl_rows, matched_bank_total, matched_gl_total, matched_total_difference, unreconciled_bank_total, unreconciled_gl_total. Set threshold to 0.50.
2) output/recon_results.csv — with header: "Bank Date,Bank Amount,Bank Desc,GL Date,GL Amount,GL Memo,Match Basis,Notes". Include a row for each matched pair/group alignment, noting the match basis (e.g., "1:1 Match", "1:n Match", "n:1 Match", "Group Match (KEY)", or "AI Semantic Match: NAME"). Put a "Diff: <amount>" note on the first row of each grouping.
3) output/unreconciled_bank.csv — with header: "date,transaction amount,description" and all unmatched bank rows.
4) output/unreconciled_gl.csv — with header: "date,amount,G/L memo" and all unmatched GL rows.

Please ensure the totals and counts in recon_summary.json reconcile with the contents of the three CSVs and that all amount fields are numeric. If you make assumptions (e.g., sign conventions), document them briefly in comments at the top of recon_summary.json.
